title: ipomoea ovatolanceolata
intro: Ipomoea ovatolanceolata, also known as the sweet potato morning glory, is a type of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. It is a perennial vine that is native to tropical Asia and is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
